message:
perf(core): HashMap side-indices for schema/view/trigger name lookup — O(n)→O(1)

`Connection.schema` is `RefCell<Vec<TableSchema>>`, and every DML
statement did a linear `iter().find(|t| t.name.eq_ignore_ascii_case(n))`
scan to resolve a name → TableSchema. For a database with N tables
and 5K mixed-OLTP ops the cost is O(N × 5000) byte-wise case-folded
comparisons.

Add three parallel `RefCell<HashMap<String, usize>>` side-indices
(`schema_by_name`, `views_by_name`, `triggers_by_name`) keyed by
lowercased name, value = index into the backing Vec. Every name
resolution becomes a single O(1) hashmap probe.

Maintenance: `rebuild_schema_indices(&self)` is called after every
schema mutation (CREATE / DROP / ALTER RENAME / reload / restore).
Rebuilding from scratch (not incremental) is cheap because DDL is
rare relative to DML, and avoids the bug surface of transient
duplicate-name windows during rename.

Invariant (debug-only, `validate_schema_index()`):
`schema_by_name.get(&k.to_ascii_lowercase()) == Some(i)` implies
`schema[i].name.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&k)`. Analogous for views /
triggers. Invariant checked at strategic points in DDL paths.

Helper: `schema_index_of(&self, name) -> Option<usize>` for the
common "lookup, then borrow to use" pattern. Callers that previously
did `self.schema.borrow().iter().find(...)` now do
`self.schema_index_of(name).and_then(|i| ...borrow().get(i)...)`.

Salvaged from worktree-agent-a39c5704 (IMPL-7b), which re-scoped the
Left-Right premise after discovering Connection is !Send+!Sync so
RwLock→Left-Right doesn't apply — but the underlying O(N) scan is
real, and a HashMap side-index closes it. Agent hit usage limit
before benchmarking; delta on Mixed OLTP to be measured post-campaign.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
